task-tracker.overview:
CLI task tracker with add, list, and complete commands.

Features:
• Add tasks
• List all tasks
• Mark tasks complete
• Exit gracefully
task-tracker.explanation: An ArrayList stores Task objects with id, title, and done flag. A menu loop reads user choice. Each task gets an auto-increment id. Marking complete sets the boolean flag; listing shows [ ] or [x] prefix.

github-user-activity.overview: Fetch a GitHub user's public events via REST API and print recent activity.
github-user-activity.explanation: HttpClient (Java 11+) sends GET to api.github.com/users/{username}/events/public. The response JSON array is parsed simply by searching for "type" fields. For production, use Jackson or Gson.

expense-tracker-cli.overview: Track daily expenses from the terminal with category and running total.
expense-tracker-cli.explanation: Each expense stores description, amount, and category. ArrayList accumulates entries. A running total updates on every add. List command prints a formatted table.

unit-converter.overview: Convert between length units: meters, kilometers, miles, and feet.
unit-converter.explanation: All conversions go through meters as the base unit. switch on from/to unit codes. User enters value and unit pair; result prints with 4 decimal precision.

github-trending-cli.overview: CLI that lists trending Java repositories from GitHub Search API.
github-trending-cli.explanation: GitHub Search API returns repositories sorted by stars. This starter parses repo full_name fields from JSON. Extend with date filters and language params.

tmdb-cli-tool.overview: Search movies by title using The Movie Database (TMDB) API.
tmdb-cli-tool.explanation: Replace YOUR_API_KEY with a free key from themoviedb.org. HttpClient calls /search/movie endpoint. Parse title fields from JSON response.
tmdb-cli-tool.runInstructions: Set TMDB API key, then:\njavac TmdbCliTool.java\njava TmdbCliTool matrix

caching-proxy.overview: Simple in-memory HTTP caching proxy for GET requests.
caching-proxy.explanation: A HashMap caches URL → response body with timestamp. On cache hit within TTL (60s), return cached body. Otherwise fetch upstream and store. Extend with HttpServer for real proxy.
caching-proxy.sampleOutput:
CACHE MISS
Design for failure...
CACHE HIT
Design for failure...

broadcast-server.overview: TCP server that broadcasts messages to all connected clients.
broadcast-server.explanation: ServerSocket listens on port 9090. Each client runs in its own thread. When a message arrives, the server writes it to every connected PrintWriter. Clients need a matching socket reader.

database-backup-utility.overview: Utility that runs mysqldump and saves a timestamped SQL backup file.
database-backup-utility.explanation: ProcessBuilder executes mysqldump with database credentials. Output redirects to backups/ folder with timestamp filename. Requires MySQL client tools installed.
database-backup-utility.runInstructions: Ensure mysqldump is installed:\njavac DatabaseBackupUtility.java\njava DatabaseBackupUtility mydb root

personal-blog.overview: Build a personal blog with Spring Boot, Thymeleaf, and a database for posts.
personal-blog.explanation: A full blog needs Spring MVC controllers, JPA entities for Post, and Thymeleaf templates. Use the Amigoscode full-stack repo as reference architecture.
personal-blog.githubLabel: Spring Boot Full Stack — Blog & CRUD patterns
personal-blog.studyNote:
1. Watch the tutorial for Spring Boot + JPA setup.
2. Clone the repo and study the Post/Article entity pattern.
3. Add create, edit, list, and delete routes.
4. Style with Thymeleaf templates.

weather-api-project.overview: REST API that fetches weather from OpenWeatherMap and returns JSON.
weather-api-project.explanation: Spring Boot @RestController exposes /weather?city=. RestTemplate or WebClient calls external weather API. Return DTO with temp, humidity, description.
weather-api-project.studyNote:
1. Create Spring Boot project with Web starter.
2. Add RestController and DTO classes.
3. Integrate OpenWeatherMap API key.
4. Test with Postman.

todo-list-api.overview: RESTful CRUD API for managing todo items with Spring Boot.
todo-list-api.explanation: Standard REST: GET /todos, POST /todos, PUT /todos/{id}, DELETE /todos/{id}. Use Spring Data JPA with H2 or PostgreSQL.
todo-list-api.githubLabel: Spring Boot Todo / Product CRUD API
todo-list-api.youtubeLabel: Build REST APIs with Spring Boot
todo-list-api.studyNote:
1. Define Todo entity with title, completed, createdAt.
2. Create TodoRepository extending JpaRepository.
3. Implement TodoController with CRUD endpoints.
4. Add validation and error handling.

blogging-platform-api.overview: REST API for a blogging platform with users, posts, and comments.
blogging-platform-api.explanation: Multi-entity API with User, Post, Comment relationships. Spring Security optional for auth. Follow layered architecture: controller → service → repository.
blogging-platform-api.youtubeLabel: REST API Design with Spring Boot
blogging-platform-api.studyNote:
1. Design entities and relationships first.
2. Build repositories and services.
3. Expose REST endpoints with proper HTTP status codes.
4. Document with Swagger/OpenAPI.

expense-tracker-api.overview: Backend API for tracking expenses with categories and monthly summaries.
expense-tracker-api.explanation: Expense entity with amount, category, date. Add aggregation endpoints for monthly totals using Spring Data queries or @Query.
expense-tracker-api.studyNote:
1. Create Expense entity and repository.
2. Build CRUD endpoints.
3. Add /summary?month= endpoint.
4. Connect PostgreSQL via application.properties.

markdown-note-app.overview: Note-taking API that stores markdown content and renders to HTML.
markdown-note-app.explanation: Store raw markdown in database. Use a library like commonmark-java to render HTML on read. REST endpoints for create, update, list notes.
markdown-note-app.studyNote:
1. Add commonmark dependency for markdown parsing.
2. Note entity with title and content fields.
3. GET /notes/{id}/html returns rendered HTML.
4. Add search by title.

url-shortening-service.overview: URL shortener API — POST long URL, GET short code redirect.
url-shortening-service.explanation: Generate unique short codes (Base62 or UUID substring). Map code → original URL in database. GET /{code} returns 302 redirect.
url-shortening-service.githubLabel: URL Shortener patterns — Baeldung Spring tutorials
url-shortening-service.youtubeLabel: Build REST APIs with Spring Boot
url-shortening-service.studyNote:
1. Study the reference repo structure.
2. Implement POST /api/shorten endpoint.
3. Handle collision on short codes.
4. Add click analytics optional.

multi-container-docker.overview: Run Spring Boot app + PostgreSQL + Redis with Docker Compose.
multi-container-docker.explanation: docker-compose.yml defines services: app, db, cache. App connects via environment variables. Learn networking, volumes, and health checks.
multi-container-docker.studyNote:
1. Write Dockerfile for Spring Boot JAR.
2. Add postgres service with volume.
3. Configure SPRING_DATASOURCE_URL env var.
4. Run docker compose up and test.

ecommerce-api-project.overview: E-commerce REST API with products, cart, and checkout flow.
ecommerce-api-project.explanation: Product catalog, Cart with line items, Order placement. Integrate Stripe or Razorpay for payments in production.
ecommerce-api-project.youtubeLabel: Product CRUD REST API — Spring Boot
ecommerce-api-project.studyNote:
1. Model Product, CartItem, Order entities.
2. Cart endpoints: add, remove, view.
3. Checkout creates Order from Cart.
4. Add payment webhook stub.

linux-server-setup.overview: Set up a secure Linux VPS — SSH, firewall, Nginx, and Java deployment.
linux-server-setup.explanation: Provision Ubuntu server. Configure UFW firewall, SSH keys, install Java 21, Nginx reverse proxy, and systemd service for Spring Boot JAR.
linux-server-setup.studyNote:
1. Create VPS on DigitalOcean/AWS free tier.
2. Harden SSH — disable password login.
3. Install Java and run JAR with systemd.
4. Configure Nginx as reverse proxy.

workout-tracker.overview: API for logging workouts, exercises, sets, and tracking progress over time.
workout-tracker.explanation: Workout → Exercise → Set hierarchy. Endpoints for daily log and progress charts. Use Spring Data JPA with date-range queries.
workout-tracker.githubLabel: Spring Boot Full Stack API patterns
workout-tracker.studyNote:
1. Design Workout and Exercise entities.
2. CRUD for logging sessions.
3. GET /progress?from=&to= for stats.
4. Optional React frontend.

image-processing-service.overview: Upload images and apply resize/thumbnail transformations.
image-processing-service.explanation: Multipart file upload endpoint. Use Java ImageIO or Thumbnailator library. Store originals and thumbnails on disk or S3.
image-processing-service.youtubeLabel: Spring Boot file upload REST API
image-processing-service.studyNote:
1. POST /upload with MultipartFile.
2. Resize to 200px thumbnail.
3. Return URLs for original and thumb.
4. Add content-type validation.

movie-reservation-system.overview: Book movie tickets with seat selection and showtime management.
movie-reservation-system.explanation: Entities: Movie, Theater, Show, Seat, Booking. Handle concurrent seat booking with optimistic locking or synchronized blocks.
movie-reservation-system.studyNote:
1. Model seats as rows/columns per show.
2. Lock seat during checkout (5 min TTL).
3. Confirm booking on payment.
4. Send confirmation response.

realtime-leaderboard.overview: Real-time game leaderboard with WebSocket updates.
realtime-leaderboard.explanation: Spring WebSocket or STOMP broadcasts score updates. Redis sorted set for O(log N) rank queries. REST endpoint for top 100.
realtime-leaderboard.studyNote:
1. POST /scores to submit score.
2. WebSocket /topic/leaderboard for live updates.
3. Redis ZADD for ranking.
4. GET /leaderboard/top100.

scalable-ecommerce-platform.overview: Microservices e-commerce: catalog, cart, order, payment services.
scalable-ecommerce-platform.explanation: Split monolith into services communicating via REST or Kafka. API Gateway routes requests. Each service owns its database.
scalable-ecommerce-platform.studyNote:
1. Study PetClinic microservices layout.
2. Extract catalog and order services.
3. Add API Gateway (Spring Cloud Gateway).
4. Docker Compose all services.
